title: rumex crispus subsp. littoreus
intro: Rumex crispus subsp. littoreus, commonly known as sea sorrel or seaside dock, is a subspecies of the plant Rumex crispus, belonging to the buckwheat family Polygonaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, making it useful in treating conditions such as kidney stones and urinary tract infections. The health benefits of Rumex crispus subsp. littoreus include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce blood sugar levels, and act as an antioxidant. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its leaves can be eaten raw in salads, adding a tangy, sour taste.
